REPORT ABUSE/NEGLECT

menu

SUPPORTING PEOPLE THROUGH STRONG PARTNERSHIPS AND
HIGH-QUALITY SERVICES.

The Allen County Board of Developmental Disabilities offers a full range of services, from in-home early intervention for babies and young children to service and support administration for school age children through adulthood.

​

We hope you spend time with the information on our website or give us a call to see if we can assist you or your family.

​

If you are looking for information regarding Intermediate Care Facilities, please see information in the links provided:

DODD Online ICF Search Tool
DODD Electronic ICF Informational Booklet

ALL ABILITY PLAYGROUND RESTROOM PROJECT

The Marimor Legacy Foundation is fundraising to build a restroom equipped with a universal changing table and a drinking fountain at the All Ability Playground on Ada Road. Support this project by donating at the link below.

THIS IS...

TAMARA. Tamara utilizes self-directed transportation to travel to work and doctor appointments, visit friends and even take a trip to the mall.

​

​

READ TAMARA'S STORY

TONY.  Tony is an incredible example of advocacy in action. Because of his positive attitude, he is able to advocate not just for himself, but for others.

​

READ TONY'S STORY

JIM. Jim utilizes a variety of technology throughout his daily life in order to live more independently and safely.
